info.name: Verify Integrations Server
info.description:
Our Express server at verify-integrations.ad-hoc.app. Covers the health check and all BigCommerce app lifecycle endpoints (OAuth install, load, uninstall).

Set `base_url` to `http://localhost:3000` for local dev.

variable.description: Switch to http://localhost:3000 for local dev
HS256 JWT signed with BC_CLIENT_SECRET. BC sends this on load/uninstall. Generate one with the 'Generate Test JWT' request below or copy from a real BC callback.

request.description: Liveness check. Returns `{ status: 'ok', ts: <epoch> }`. No auth required.

item.name: BigCommerce — OAuth Lifecycle
item.description:
These three endpoints are called by BigCommerce during app installation, app load (merchant opens the app in the control panel), and app uninstall.

`/auth` receives a plain `code` query param. `/load` and `/uninstall` receive a `signed_payload_jwt` — an HS256 JWT signed with `BC_CLIENT_SECRET`.

query.description: One-time authorization code from BigCommerce. Only valid for a few minutes and only usable once — copy from the real BC redirect.

request.description:
Triggered when a merchant installs the app from the BC Marketplace. BC redirects to this URL with a one-time `code`. The server exchanges it for an access token at `login.bigcommerce.com/oauth2/token`, stores the token in `data/tokens.json`, and redirects to `/bigcommerce/admin/`.

**You can only test this with a real code from a real BC install flow** — codes expire quickly and are single-use. Use this request to replay a code you captured from server logs.

query.description: HS256 JWT signed with BC_CLIENT_SECRET. Claims: { sub: 'stores/<hash>', iss: '<clientId>', iat, exp, user: { id, email } }

request.description:
Called by BC every time the merchant opens the app in the control panel (inside an iframe). Verifies the JWT, looks up the stored access token, injects `window.AdHocAdminConfig` into `dist/admin/index.html`, and returns the SPA HTML.

Headers: `Cache-Control: no-store`, `Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self' https://*.bigcommerce.com`.

To test locally: generate a JWT with `jsonwebtoken` (or jwt.io) using your `BC_CLIENT_SECRET` and the claims above. The store hash must already exist in `data/tokens.json` (i.e. the store must have completed auth first).

query.description: HS256 JWT signed with BC_CLIENT_SECRET. Claims: { sub: 'stores/<hash>', iat, exp }

request.description:
Called by BC when a merchant uninstalls the app. Verifies the JWT and deletes the token record from `data/tokens.json`. Must return 200 or BC retries.

To test locally: generate a JWT the same way as `/load` (sub + iat/exp only — no user claim needed).
Serves `dist/admin/index.html` statically. No JWT check here — auth happens at `/bigcommerce/load`. Useful to check the admin build is deployed correctly.
